model.label: DeepSeek V3.2
model.description: DeepSeek • General-purpose V-series model optimized for reasoning, coding, and efficient inference.

pick.rationale: Arizona's elite efficiency on both ends of the floor, ranking 3rd nationally with a top-10 offense and top-3 defense, creates a substantial mismatch against Utah State. The Wildcats' 31-2 record was built against a rigorous Big 12 schedule, providing high-major experience that the Aggies' Mountain West campaign cannot match. While Utah State is a solid team with good metrics, they lack the firepower to keep pace with Arizona's balanced attack and defensive intensity over 40 minutes.

reasoningStep.title: Compare team ratings and tournament performance
reasoningStep.summary: Arizona's elite top-3 efficiency on both offense and defense, coupled with a 31-2 record from the rigorous Big 12, provides a significant advantage over Utah State, which is a solid team but ranks around 30th in efficiency metrics.
evidence: Arizona's Torvik rank: 3, adjoe rank: 10, adjde rank: 3, record 31-2 (Big 12). Utah State's KenPom rank: 30, NET rank: 26, record 28-6, Mountain West champion, top-25 rebounding margin (per bracket breakdown).

toolCalls.summary: Returned combined Torvik/KenPom ratings for 2 teams.

result.text: Bracket Breakdown: The West Region | Hammer and Rails Skip to main content SB Nation SB Nation logo A Purdue Boilermakers Community Hammer and Rails March 18, 2026 Hammer and Rails Notifications Notifications The Feed Sections Purdue Men’s Basketball Purdue Football Purdue Men’s Basketball Recruiting The H&R Column Purdue Football Recruiting Betting FanDuel College Football Odds FanDuel College Basketball Odds Purdue Football Odds Purdue Basketball Odds College Football Picks and Predictions College Basketball Picks and Predictions Newsletters About Navigation Drawer Login / Sign Up close Close Search The Feed Sections Expand Purdue Men’s Basketball Purdue Football Purdue Men’s Basketball Recruiting The H&R Column Purdue Football Recruiting Betting Expand FanDuel College Football Odds FanDuel College Basketball Odds Purdue Football Odds Purdue Basketball Odds College Football Picks and Predictions College Basketball Picks and Predictions Newsletters About Community Guidelines All communities Facebook Twitter Youtube RSS Hammer and Rails Bracket Breakdown: The West Region Notifications Notifications Purdue Men's Basketball Bracket Breakdown: The West Region by Jed Wilkinson Mar 16, 2026, 11:30 AM UTC Facebook Link Following their victory in the B1G Tournament against the Michigan Wolverines, Purdue was able to elevate themselves into the 2-seed in the West Region. The Boilers set themselves up as well as they ever have for a run in the NCAA Tourney and will first square off against 15-seeded Queens. With the first stop in St. Louis, if Purdue wins their round-1 matchup, they will face off against either 7-seeded Miami (FL) or 10-seeded Missouri. Let’s take a look at the West Region overall. No. 1 Arizona (32-2): After capturing the Big 12 tournament title, the Wildcats enter the tournament as one of the favorites to win the whole tourney. The Wildcats have a NET ranking of #3 and a matching KenPom rank of #2 with the 5th best adjusted offense and 3rd best adjusted defense. They boast 16 Quadrant 1 victories, led by potential top-five NBA pick Brayden Burries. Arizona is one of the most well balanced teams in the country, similarly statistically to Michigan and Duke. No. 2 Purdue (27-8): Purdue secured the No. 2 seed by winning the Big Ten Tournament with a dominating performance against Michigan. They hold a NET ranking of #9 and are #8 in KenPom, supported by 11 wins over the NET top 100. The Boilers had a mid-season struggle but used the B1G Tourney to vault themselves into their 2-seed with a renewed effort on defense and the best offense in the country. No. 3 Gonzaga (30-3): The Zags swept through the WCC to finish with 30 wins. The Bulldogs are ranked in the N ET at #6 and a KenPom rank of #10. Despite injuries to star Braden Huff and Jalen Warley, they maintain 7 wins against the top 100 and one of the best defenses in the country. They don’t have the guards they once did but their frontline, when healthy, is one of the best in the coun

You have live tools. Use them before making a pick when they can reduce uncertainty.

They boast 16 Quadrant 1 victories, led by potential top-five NBA pick Brayden Burries. Arizona is one of the most well balanced teams in the country, similarly statistically to Michigan and Duke. No. 2 Purdue (27-8): Purdue secured the No. 2 seed by winning the Big Ten Tournament with a dominating performance against Michigan. They hold a NET ranking of #9 and are #8 in KenPom, supported by 11 wins over the NET top 100. The Boilers had a mid-season struggle but used the B1G Tourney to vault themselves into their 2-seed with a renewed effort on defense and the best offense in the country. No. 3 Gonzaga (30-3): The Zags swept through the WCC to finish with 30 wins. The Bulldogs are ranked in the N ET at #6 and a KenPom rank of #10. Despite injuries to star Braden Huff and Jalen Warley, they maintain 7 wins against the top 100 and one of the best defenses in the country. They don’t have the guards they once did but their frontline, when healthy, is one of the best in the coun
